Code fragment: public final TableField<ChUsersRecord, Integer> USER_NO = createField(DSL.name("USER_NO"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic auto-increment primary key (USER_NO) even though USER_EMAIL already has a unique constraint and could serve as a natural primary key. Consider using USER_EMAIL as the primary key since email addresses are naturally unique identifiers for users, or rename USER_NO to something more descriptive like CH_USERS_ID.

Using selectFrom(CH_USERS) fetches all columns from the CH_USERS table, but only USER_NO, USER_EMAIL, and USER_NAME are actually used. Replace with an explicit select statement: dsl.select(CH_USERS.USER_NO, CH_USERS.USER_EMAIL, CH_USERS.USER_NAME).from(CH_USERS).

Using LIKE with wildcards on both sides ('%keyword%') for searching recipe titles performs poorly on large datasets as it cannot use indexes. Consider using a full-text search engine like PostgreSQL's tsvector/tsquery, Elasticsearch, or a dedicated search service.
